step_by_step_thinking:
I concur with the assessment provided by agent1. The quick development and subsequent disappearance of the rash, coupled with its reappearance shortly after reintroducing the same medications, fits well into the pattern seen with Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS)/Toxic Epidermal Necrolysis (TEN). Recurrence tends to occur quickly once re-exposure happens due to pre-existing sensitization. Thus, while any time frame could theoretically apply depending on individual factors, predicting around 2-4 weeks makes sense considering common clinical experience.
